text: What is the primary role of a goalkeeper?
explanation: The goalkeeper's main job is to prevent the opposing team from scoring.

text: Which part of the body can a goalkeeper use inside the penalty area that outfield players cannot?
explanation: Goalkeepers can use their hands within their own penalty area.

text: What color jersey does a goalkeeper typically wear?
choices: A different color from all other players
explanation: Goalkeepers must wear a distinguishing color from teammates, opponents, and referees.

text: Which legendary Italian goalkeeper is known as 'Superman'?
explanation: Gianluigi Buffon earned the nickname 'Superman' for his extraordinary saves.

text: Which goalkeeper has won the most World Cup Golden Glove awards?
explanation: Manuel Neuer won the Golden Glove at the 2014 World Cup.

text: What is the 6-yard box also known as?
explanation: The 6-yard box is officially called the goal area.

text: Which goalkeeper scored the most career goals?
explanation: Rogério Ceni scored over 130 goals in his career, mostly from free kicks and penalties.

text: Who was the first goalkeeper to win the Ballon d'Or?
explanation: Lev Yashin won the Ballon d'Or in 1963, the only goalkeeper ever to do so.

text: What famous save technique involves diving with a scorpion-like kick behind the back?
explanation: René Higuita popularized the scorpion kick save in 1995 against England.

text: Can a goalkeeper handle a deliberate back-pass from a teammate?
choices: No, it results in an indirect free kick
Yes, but only in extra time
Only if the ball crosses the 6-yard box
explanation: The back-pass rule (1992) prohibits goalkeepers from handling deliberate passes from teammates.

text: Which goalkeeper captained Spain to their 2010 World Cup victory?
explanation: Iker Casillas was Spain's captain during their triumphant 2010 World Cup campaign.

text: What is the standard number of defenders in a 4-3-3 formation?
explanation: The 4-3-3 formation uses 4 defenders.

text: What does a 'clean sheet' mean?
choices: A match where no fouls were committed
A match where the team did not concede a goal
A match with no yellow cards
explanation: A clean sheet means the team conceded zero goals in the match.

text: Which defender position plays in the center of the back line?
explanation: Center backs play in the middle of the defensive line.

text: What is an offside trap?
choices: A defensive strategy where the line pushes up to catch attackers offside
explanation: The offside trap involves defenders stepping forward in unison before a pass to catch attackers offside.

text: Which Spanish defender holds the record for most international caps for Spain?
explanation: Sergio Ramos holds Spain's record with 180 international caps.

text: What is a 'sweeper' (libero) in football?
choices: A defender who plays behind the main defensive line
explanation: The sweeper plays behind the defensive line and 'sweeps up' any balls that get past the main defenders.

text: Which Italian defensive system is famous for its tight man-marking?
explanation: Catenaccio is an Italian defensive tactic emphasizing tight man-marking and a sweeper.

text: Which defender won the 2006 Ballon d'Or?
explanation: Fabio Cannavaro won the 2006 Ballon d'Or after captaining Italy to the World Cup title.

text: Which defender has scored the most goals in Champions League history?
explanation: Sergio Ramos holds the record for most goals by a defender in Champions League history.

text: Who is the youngest player to captain a Champions League winning team?
explanation: Paolo Maldini captained AC Milan in Champions League victories at a remarkably young age.

text: What is a 'tackle' in football?
choices: An attempt to take the ball from an opponent
explanation: A tackle is the act of challenging an opponent to win possession of the ball.

text: Which country is Paolo Maldini from?
explanation: Paolo Maldini is Italian and spent his entire career at AC Milan.

text: Which defender has made the most appearances in World Cup history?
explanation: Lothar Matthäus holds the record for most World Cup appearances with 25 matches.

text: In what situation is a penalty kick awarded?
choices: Foul inside the penalty area by the defending team
explanation: A penalty kick is awarded when the defending team commits a foul inside their own penalty area.

text: What is a 'last-man' foul?
choices: A foul by the last defender that denies a clear goal-scoring opportunity
explanation: A last-man foul denies an obvious goal-scoring opportunity and typically results in a red card.

text: Which left-back is famous for his powerful free kicks and played for Real Madrid and Brazil?
explanation: Roberto Carlos was known for his incredible left-foot free kicks and played for Real Madrid from 1996 to 2007.

text: How many defenders typically form a back three?
explanation: A back three uses 3 center-backs, often with wing-backs providing width.

text: What does 'marking' mean in defensive play?
choices: Staying close to an opponent to prevent them receiving the ball
explanation: Marking means closely guarding an opponent to prevent them from receiving or playing the ball effectively.

text: Which defender played 647 consecutive league matches for AC Milan without missing a game?
explanation: Franco Baresi was renowned for his consistency and longevity at AC Milan.

text: Which nation did Franz Beckenbauer represent?
explanation: Franz Beckenbauer represented West Germany and is considered one of the greatest defenders ever.

text: What is the main role of a central midfielder?
choices: Link defense and attack, control the tempo
explanation: Central midfielders connect defense to attack and dictate the pace of the game.

text: What does 'possession' mean in football?
choices: How many goals a team scores
The percentage of time a team has the ball
explanation: Possession refers to the percentage of time a team controls the ball during a match.

text: Which country invented 'tiki-taka' style of play?
explanation: Spain and FC Barcelona are credited with developing the tiki-taka passing style.

text: Which midfielder is known as 'The Architect' and played for Barcelona and Spain?
explanation: Xavi Hernández was nicknamed 'The Architect' for his ability to orchestrate play.

text: What is a 'box-to-box' midfielder?
choices: A midfielder who covers the full length of the pitch, defending and attacking
explanation: A box-to-box midfielder contributes at both ends of the pitch, covering penalty area to penalty area.

text: Which French midfielder won 3 consecutive Ballon d'Or awards in the 1980s?
explanation: Michel Platini won the Ballon d'Or in 1983, 1984, and 1985.

text: Who scored the famous volley goal in the 2002 Champions League final?
explanation: Zinedine Zidane scored a stunning left-foot volley in the 2002 Champions League final against Bayer Leverkusen.

text: Which midfielder has the most assists in Champions League history?
explanation: Lionel Messi holds the record for most assists in Champions League history.

text: What is a 'through ball'?
choices: A pass played into space behind the defense for a teammate to run onto
explanation: A through ball splits the defense and allows an attacker to run onto it in a goal-scoring position.

text: Which German midfielder captained Bayern Munich and Germany, known for his versatility?
explanation: Bastian Schweinsteiger captained both Bayern Munich and Germany and could play multiple positions.

text: Who holds the record for most La Liga assists of all time?
explanation: Lionel Messi holds the record for the most assists in La Liga history.

text: What is a 'holding midfielder' (number 6)?
choices: A defensive midfielder who shields the back line
explanation: The holding midfielder sits in front of the defense, breaking up opponent attacks and distributing the ball.

text: Which midfielder scored in two different World Cup finals?
explanation: Zinedine Zidane scored in the 1998 World Cup final (2 goals) and the 2006 final (1 goal via penalty).

text: What does 'dribbling' mean in football?
choices: Passing the ball to a teammate
Running with the ball at your feet while maintaining control
explanation: Dribbling is the skill of moving the ball forward while keeping it under close control.

text: Which midfielder scored the winning goal in the 2010 World Cup final?
explanation: Andrés Iniesta scored the winning goal in extra time against Netherlands in the 2010 World Cup final.

text: What is a 'hat-trick'?
explanation: A hat-trick is when a player scores three goals in a single match.

text: Which player has scored the most goals in FIFA World Cup history?
explanation: Miroslav Klose scored 16 goals across four World Cups for Germany.

text: What is the 'Golden Boot' award?
choices: Award for the top scorer in a tournament
explanation: The Golden Boot is awarded to the top scorer in a football tournament.

text: Who won the most Ballon d'Or awards?
explanation: Lionel Messi has won the Ballon d'Or 8 times, more than any other player.

text: Which striker scored 91 goals in a calendar year (2012)?
explanation: Lionel Messi scored 91 goals in 2012, breaking Gerd Müller's record of 85 goals in 1972.

text: What is the 'False 9' position?
choices: A winger who plays as a center back
A center forward who drops deep into midfield to create space
A defender who acts as a striker
explanation: The False 9 drops deep from the striker position to link play and create space for teammates.

text: Who scored the 'Hand of God' goal?
explanation: Diego Maradona scored the 'Hand of God' goal against England in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final.

text: Which Brazilian striker won the World Cup twice (1994 and 2002)?
explanation: Ronaldo Nazário won the World Cup in 1994 (as a squad member) and 2002 (as the star striker).

text: What is a 'volley' in football?
choices: Kicking the ball while it is in the air before it bounces
explanation: A volley is striking the ball while it is still in the air.

text: Which club did Erling Haaland join from Borussia Dortmund?
explanation: Erling Haaland transferred from Borussia Dortmund to Manchester City in 2022.

text: Who is the all-time top scorer in Champions League history?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for the most goals in Champions League history.

text: Which country did the legendary striker Pelé represent?
explanation: Pelé played for Brazil and won three FIFA World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970).

text: Which striker scored the fastest hat-trick in Premier League history?
explanation: Sadio Mané scored the fastest Premier League hat-trick in 2 minutes 56 seconds for Southampton against Aston Villa in 2015.

text: What does 'offside' mean for an attacker?
choices: Being behind the ball when it is passed
Being nearer to the opponent's goal line than the second-last defender when the ball is played
explanation: An attacker is offside if they are nearer to the goal line than both the ball and the second-last defender.

text: Which World Cup saw the most goals scored in a single tournament by one player (13 goals)?
explanation: Just Fontaine scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup, a record that still stands.

text: Which defender has won the most Champions League titles?
explanation: Marcelo won 5 Champions League titles with Real Madrid.

text: Which midfielder has scored the most goals in World Cup finals history?
explanation: Pelé scored in multiple World Cup finals, accumulating the most final goals.

text: What is a 'header clearance'?
choices: Using the head to clear the ball away from danger in the defensive area
explanation: A header clearance is when a defender uses their head to direct the ball away from their goal.
